nativeRange.habitat: Shallow reef and rocky subtidal zones, intertidal to subtidal, attached to hard substrate or as epiphyte on larger seaweeds

avoidWith: herbivorous fish that graze heavily on macroalgae (e.g. tangs, rabbitfish in small tanks)

imageCandidates.notes: Product image from Mosaic Macros listing for Blue Scroll Algae (Padina sp.). Sole hobby-trade source for this specialty entry.

summary: A specialty brown macroalga (family Dictyotaceae) forming thin, scrolling fan-shaped fronds with concentric banding that exhibit a blue iridescent shimmer under full-spectrum reef lighting. Lightly calcareous; suited to display reef tanks.
careNotes: Blue Scroll (Padina sp.) is a specialty ornamental brown macroalga in the family Dictyotaceae, order Dictyotales — one of the very few calcareous members of the brown algae. Its thallus grows as a thin, fan- or scroll-shaped frond with characteristic concentric banding and marginal hair zones, typical of the Padina genus. The distinctive blue iridescent shimmer is a structural coloration effect visible under full-spectrum aquarium lighting; the alga tends to appear predominantly brown under lower or narrow-spectrum illumination. The species-level identification is not resolved from the trade listing (sold as Padina sp.); the Padina genus encompasses numerous tropical and subtropical marine species distributed widely across the Indo-Pacific and Atlantic. Standard reef water parameters are generally advised: temperature around 24–26°C, pH 8.1–8.4, alkalinity 8–12 dKH, and specific gravity 1.023–1.026. Medium lighting is recommended to elicit the characteristic blue shimmer; inadequate illumination tends to suppress the iridescence. Husbandry values for growth rate (medium) and nutrient uptake (low) are extrapolated from published Dictyotaceae family-level care standards, as species-specific captive data is limited in the broader hobby literature. Propagation in the aquarium is typically achieved by fragmentation of healthy frond sections. As a display specimen, placement on exposed rockwork in moderate flow is generally recommended. Note that Padina is lightly calcareous — it deposits a thin layer of calcium carbonate on its fronds, which may contribute minimally to alkalinity consumption in the tank. Source coverage caveat: this entry is based on a single specialty trade source (Mosaic Macros) plus family-level taxonomic extrapolation from Wikipedia and published Dictyotaceae ecology. Mosaic Macros is the sole known hobby-trade source for this specific form at the time of this entry. Per Mosaic care guide guidance: as a red or brown alga, this species will MELT at the base if buried in sand — attach to rock via superglue, zip-tie/fishing line, or wedging instead. (Sand-bed acceptance per Mosaic is limited to Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea genera, plus some calcified Dasycladales.)
breedingNotes: Propagation in captivity is generally accomplished by fragmentation — breaking or cutting healthy frond sections and wedging them among rockwork or attaching to a plug. Sexual reproduction (alternating gametophyte–sporophyte lifecycle typical of brown algae) has not been documented in hobby aquaria for this species.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby-trade source for this specialty form. Product listing for 'Blue Scroll Algae' (Padina sp.). Provides identification, imagery, morphology description (blue shimmer under full-spectrum lighting, brown macroalga with scroll form), and general reef compatibility context.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Padina (alga)
additional.notes: Used for taxonomic classification (Dictyotaceae / Dictyotales / Phaeophyceae), morphology confirmation (fan-shaped fronds, concentric banding, lightly calcareous), and native range (tropical to temperate, Indo-Pacific to Atlantic, intertidal to ~30 m typical photic zone). No aquarium husbandry parameters provided.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
